plan_metadata.name: Feature  DA No Action EK Fail With Oversize And Incorrect Format

steps.description: Select the "Declarative Agent – Agents for Microsoft 365 Copilot" option in the "New Project" dropdown within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "No Action" option in the dropdown menu within the "Create Declarative Agent" popup in Visual Studio Code's Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension.
Click the "Default Folder" option in the "Workspace Folder" selection dropdown at the top of the Visual Studio Code window.

parameters.sample:
=== Generated Script ===
Language: bash

```bash
FILE="/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_uY7ve/testing.docx"
DIR="/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_uY7ve"
SIZE_MB=2
SIZE_BYTES=$((SIZE_MB * 1024 * 1024))

# Ensure directory exists
if [ ! -d "$DIR" ]; then
  mkdir -p "$DIR"
fi

# Create a large dummy file and name it .docx
head -c "$SIZE_BYTES" /dev/urandom > "$FILE"

# Show stats to verify size
ls -lh "$FILE"
stat "$FILE"
```

steps.description: @code create testing.docx file more than 1mb at path: '/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}'
Use the keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Shift+P in Visual Studio Code to open the command palette, allowing you to execute commands within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension interface.

Type the text 'add capability' into the Visual Studio Code Command Palette to search for the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Add Capability" command, within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension interface.
Press the Enter key to select the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Add Capability" command from the Visual Studio Code command palette after searching for ">add capability".
Click the "Embedded Knowledge" option in the "Add Capability" dropdown within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code, to embed files directly as grounding information.

Click the "manifest.json" file entry in the "Select Teams manifest.json File" dialog within Visual Studio Code to choose the file located at `/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_tecaN/appPackage`.
Click the "Browse..." button in the "Add embedded knowledge files" file picker dialog at the top of the Visual Studio Code window to open the file selection dialog.
Click the "testing.docx" file entry in the file selection dialog to select the document.
Click the "Open" button in the bottom-right corner of the file selection dialog to confirm opening the selected file "testing.docx".
Click the "Add" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit confirmation dialog within Visual Studio Code to proceed with modifying files in the app package folder.
Click the "Provision" option under the "LIFECYCLE" section in the left sidebar of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code.
@assertion there's failed message: "exceeds the maximum file size of 1MB"

Language: bash

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -e

DIR="/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_uY7ve"
FILE="$DIR/testing.png"

if [ ! -d "$DIR" ]; then
  echo "Directory does not exist: $DIR"
  exit 1
fi

touch "$FILE"
if [ -f "$FILE" ]; then
  echo "File created: $FILE"
else
  echo "Failed to create file: $FILE"
  exit 1
fi
```

steps.description: @code create testing.png file at path: '/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}'

Use the keyboard shortcut **Ctrl+Shift+P** in Visual Studio Code to open the Command Palette, while inside the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" workspace with the "Provision" lifecycle step highlighted in the left sidebar.

Type the text "add capability" into the Visual Studio Code command palette to search for the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Add Capability" command within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension interface.
Press the Enter key to select "Microsoft 365 Agents: Add Capability" from the VS Code command palette after searching for ">add capability".
Click the "Web Search" option in the "Add Capability" dropdown within the Microsoft 365 Agents pane of the Visual Studio Code extension interface.
Click the "manifest.json" file entry in the file selection dropdown within the "Select Teams manifest.json File" dialog of Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Browse..." button in the "Add embedded knowledge files" dialog within Visual Studio Code, initiating file selection for uploading knowledge files.
Click the "testing.png" file in the file selection dialog of a Linux desktop application, targeting the file list in the workspace directory.
Click the "Open" button at the bottom-right of a file selection dialog to confirm file selection in a desktop application interface.
Click the "Add" button in the confirmation dialog of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within the Visual Studio Code interface to proceed with adding a capability to the app package.

Open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code by pressing Ctrl+Shift+P from the main workspace, where the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension is active and the "Provision" lifecycle item is selected in the side menu.
Type text 'provision' into the Visual Studio Code command palette to search for the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Provision" command within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension interface.
Press the Enter key to select "Microsoft 365 Agents: Provision" in the Visual Studio Code command palette after typing ">provision".
Click the "dev" environment option in the "Select an environment" dropdown within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension sidebar of Visual Studio Code.
@assertion there's failed message: "file is not supported."
